# cratons-lockfile

Lockfile format and operations for the Cratons package manager.

## Overview

This crate handles the `cratons.lock` lockfile, which pins all dependencies to exact versions with integrity hashes for reproducible builds.

## Lockfile Format

```toml
# This file is automatically generated by Cratons.
# Do not edit manually.

version = 1
generated = "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
cratons-version = "0.1.0"

[manifest-hash]
algorithm = "blake3"
value = "abc123..."

[[packages]]
name = "lodash"
version = "4.17.21"
ecosystem = "npm"
source = "https://registry.npmjs.org/lodash/-/lodash-4.17.21.tgz"
integrity = "sha256-abc123..."
direct = true

[packages.resolved-hash]
algorithm = "blake3"
value = "def456..."

[[packages.dependencies]]
name = "some-dep"
version = "1.0.0"

[[packages]]
name = "requests"
version = "2.31.0"
ecosystem = "pypi"
source = "https://files.pythonhosted.org/..."
integrity = "sha256-xyz789..."
direct = true
```

## Usage

### Creating a Lockfile

```rust
use cratons_lockfile::{Lockfile, LockedPackage, DependencyRef};
use cratons_core::{ContentHash, Ecosystem};

// Create new lockfile with manifest hash
let manifest_hash = ContentHash::blake3("manifest-content".to_string());
let mut lockfile = Lockfile::new(manifest_hash);

// Add a locked package
lockfile.add_package(LockedPackage {
    name: "lodash".to_string(),
    version: "4.17.21".to_string(),
    ecosystem: Ecosystem::Npm,
    source: "https://registry.npmjs.org/lodash/-/lodash-4.17.21.tgz".to_string(),
    integrity: "sha256-abc123".to_string(),
    resolved_hash: ContentHash::blake3("lodash-content".to_string()),
    direct: true,
    features: vec![],
    dependencies: vec![
        DependencyRef::new("some-dep", "1.0.0"),
    ],
});
```

### Loading and Saving

```rust
use cratons_lockfile::{Lockfile, LOCKFILE_NAME};

// Load existing lockfile
let lockfile = Lockfile::load("cratons.lock")?;

// Or find it relative to manifest
let lockfile = Lockfile::load(manifest_dir.join(LOCKFILE_NAME))?;

// Save lockfile
lockfile.save("cratons.lock")?;
```

### Checking Freshness

```rust
use cratons_lockfile::Lockfile;
use cratons_core::ContentHash;

let lockfile = Lockfile::load("cratons.lock")?;
let current_manifest_hash = ContentHash::blake3(manifest_content);

if lockfile.is_fresh(&current_manifest_hash) {
    println!("Lockfile is up to date");
} else {
    println!("Manifest changed, need to re-resolve");
}
```

### Querying Packages

```rust
use cratons_lockfile::Lockfile;
use cratons_core::Ecosystem;

let lockfile = Lockfile::load("cratons.lock")?;

// Find a specific package
if let Some(pkg) = lockfile.find_package("lodash", Ecosystem::Npm) {
    println!("lodash@{}", pkg.version);
    println!("Source: {}", pkg.source);
    println!("Integrity: {}", pkg.integrity);
}

// Get all packages for an ecosystem
for pkg in lockfile.packages_for_ecosystem(Ecosystem::Npm) {
    println!("{}: {}", pkg.name, pkg.version);
}

// Get direct dependencies only
for pkg in lockfile.direct_packages() {
    println!("Direct: {}@{}", pkg.name, pkg.version);
}

// Total package count
println!("Total packages: {}", lockfile.package_count());
```

### Artifact Cache

The lockfile also tracks build artifact cache mappings:

```rust
use cratons_lockfile::{Lockfile, ArtifactCache};
use cratons_core::ContentHash;

let mut lockfile = Lockfile::load("cratons.lock")?;

// Get cached artifact
if let Some(cache) = lockfile.get_artifact_cache("input-hash") {
    println!("Cached output: {}", cache.output);
    println!("Built at: {}", cache.built);
}

// Update cache
lockfile.update_artifact_cache(
    "input-hash".to_string(),
    ArtifactCache::new(ContentHash::blake3("output".to_string())),
);

lockfile.save("cratons.lock")?;
```

## Types

### LockedPackage

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | `String` | Package name |
| `version` | `String` | Exact version |
| `ecosystem` | `Ecosystem` | npm, pypi, crates, go, maven |
| `source` | `String` | Download URL |
| `integrity` | `String` | Integrity hash (sha256-...) |
| `resolved_hash` | `ContentHash` | Blake3 content hash |
| `direct` | `bool` | Is direct dependency? |
| `features` | `Vec<String>` | Enabled features |
| `dependencies` | `Vec<DependencyRef>` | Transitive dependencies |

### DependencyRef

Simple reference to a dependency:

```rust
pub struct DependencyRef {
    pub name: String,
    pub version: String,
}
```

## Constants

- `LOCKFILE_VERSION`: Current lockfile format version (1)
- `LOCKFILE_NAME`: Default filename (`cratons.lock`)
